mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, mechanically depressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in >65% of periods when FEDFUNDS increases, or rolling Rank IC remains below 0.02 over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 4.0%, labor market slack signals weakening aggregate demand and rising recession probability, causing equity investors to de-risk and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in >55% of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power when UNRATE remains above 4.0% for more than 6 consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: The joint occurrence of rising real auto sales and declining federal funds rates indicates robust consumer credit expansion and strong discretionary spending, which directly boosts corporate revenue expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The interaction term yields a negative or statistically insignificant coefficient in out-of-sample testing, or positive returns fail to materialize when RSAFS rises while FEDFUNDS falls.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing capacity utilization and rising corporate output, which translates into immediate positive earnings revisions and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >60% of periods following an INDPRO acceleration, or the signal reverses direction during supply-constrained inflationary periods.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls indicates a strengthening labor market and rising household income, which supports consumer spending and corporate profit growth, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rolling Rank IC falls below 0.01 over a 3-year window, or 20-day returns are negative in >58% of periods with positive payroll growth.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 250.0, persistent inflationary pressures force central banks to maintain restrictive policy, compressing equity multiples and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in >60% of triggered instances, or the threshold effect disappears when CPIAUCSL growth decelerates despite the level remaining above 250.0.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in housing starts and industrial production signals broad-based economic strength across residential and manufacturing sectors, boosting aggregate corporate earnings forecasts and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative returns in >55% of co-occurring periods, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Positive quarterly GDP growth confirms macroeconomic expansion and validates corporate revenue trajectories, which sustains equity risk premiums and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in >65% of periods following positive GDPC1 growth, or the signal fails to predict returns during stagflationary episodes where GDP grows but inflation spikes.
